vespa 0.4.0

Deform a triangular surface mesh

mesh_deformation.Rd

Deforms a triangulated surface mesh by moving a set of control vertices to specified target positions, propagating the deformation across the surface using CGAL's as-rigid-as-possible (ARAP) or smoothed rotation enhanced ARAP (SRE-ARAP) algorithms.

Usage

mesh_deformation(
  mesh,
  control_ids,
  target_coords,
  roi_ids = integer(0),
  mode = c("smooth", "sre_arap"),
  sre_alpha = 0.02,
  n_iterations = 5L,
  tolerance = 1e-04
)

Arguments

mesh

A mesh3d object.

control_ids

Integer vector of 1-based vertex indices that act as handles.

target_coords

Numeric matrix with length(control_ids) rows and 3 columns (x, y, z). Target world-space positions for each handle.

roi_ids

Integer vector of 1-based vertex indices forming the region of influence. Use integer(0) (the default) to let the filter determine the region automatically.

mode

Deformation algorithm: "smooth" (ARAP, default) or "sre_arap" (Smoothed Rotation Enhanced ARAP).

sre_alpha

Blending weight for SRE-ARAP (0 = pure ARAP, 1 = pure shape). Default 0.02.

n_iterations

Number of optimisation iterations. Default 5L.

tolerance

Convergence tolerance. Default 1e-4.

Value

A mesh3d object with the deformed surface.

Examples

# \donttest{
f <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa")
mesh <- read_stl(f)
orig   <- mesh$vb[1:3, 1]
target <- matrix(orig + c(0.1, 0, 0), nrow = 1)
result <- mesh_deformation(mesh, control_ids = 1L, target_coords = target)
# }
